audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Bethel return, foreign gods purification, terror of God, Deborah's burial, Jacob/Israel name reaffirmed, Rachel's death, Benjamin naming, Reuben/Bilhah household violation notice, twelve sons list, and Isaac's death.

verses.restored: The Divine said to Jacob, Rise, go up to Bethel and dwell there. Make there an altar to the Divine who appeared to you when you fled from the face of Esau your brother.

familiar.text: And God said unto Jacob, Arise, go up to Bethel...

notes.title: Return to Bethel
notes.body: Jacob is sent back to the vow-place where the promise of return began.

verses.restored: Jacob said to his house and to all who were with him, Remove the foreign gods that are among you, purify yourselves, and change your garments.

familiar.text: Then Jacob said unto his household... Put away the strange gods...

verses.restored: Let us rise and go up to Bethel. There I will make an altar to the Divine who answered me in the day of my distress and was with me in the way that I went.

familiar.text: And let us arise, and go up to Bethel...

verses.restored: They gave Jacob all the foreign gods that were in their hand and the rings that were in their ears, and Jacob hid them under the terebinth that was near Shechem.

familiar.text: And they gave unto Jacob all the strange gods...

verses.restored: They journeyed, and a terror of the Divine was upon the cities around them, so they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.

familiar.text: And they journeyed: and the terror of God was upon the cities...

verses.restored: Jacob came to Luz, that is Bethel, in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him.

familiar.text: So Jacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan...

verses.restored: He built an altar there and called the place El-Bethel, because there the Divine had been revealed to him when he fled from the face of his brother.

familiar.text: And he built there an altar, and called the place Elbethel...

notes.title: El-Bethel
notes.body: El-Bethel means God/the Divine of Bethel.

verses.restored: Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died, and she was buried below Bethel under the oak. He called its name Allon-bacuth.

familiar.text: But Deborah Rebekah's nurse died...

notes.title: Allon-bacuth
notes.body: The name means oak of weeping.

verses.restored: The Divine appeared to Jacob again when he came from Paddan-Aram, and blessed him.

familiar.text: And God appeared unto Jacob again...

verses.restored: The Divine said to him, Your name is Jacob; your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name. And he called his name Israel.

familiar.text: And God said unto him, Thy name is Jacob...

verses.restored: The Divine said to him, I am El Shaddai. Be fruitful and multiply. A nation and an assembly of nations shall come from you, and kings shall go out from your loins.

familiar.text: And God said unto him, I am God Almighty...

verses.restored: The land that I gave to Abraham and to Isaac I will give to you, and to your seed after you I will give the land.

familiar.text: And the land which I gave Abraham and Isaac...

verses.restored: The Divine went up from him in the place where he had spoken with him.

familiar.text: And God went up from him...

verses.restored: Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone, and poured a drink offering on it and poured oil on it.

familiar.text: And Jacob set up a pillar...

verses.restored: Jacob called the name of the place where the Divine had spoken with him Bethel.

familiar.text: And Jacob called the name of the place... Bethel.

verses.restored: They journeyed from Bethel, and while there was still some distance to Ephrath, Rachel gave birth, and her labor was hard.

familiar.text: And they journeyed from Bethel... Rachel travailed...

verses.restored: When her labor was hard, the midwife said to her, Do not fear, for this also is a son for you.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when she was in hard labour...

verses.restored: As her life was departing, for she died, she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, as her soul was in departing...

notes.title: Ben-oni / Benjamin
notes.body: Rachel names him son of my sorrow/strength; Jacob renames him son of the right hand/south.

verses.restored: Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath, that is Bethlehem.

familiar.text: And Rachel died, and was buried in the way to Ephrath...

verses.restored: Jacob set up a pillar over her burial place; it is the pillar of Rachel’s burial place to this day.

familiar.text: And Jacob set a pillar upon her grave...

verses.restored: Israel journeyed and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.

familiar.text: And Israel journeyed, and spread his tent beyond the tower of Edar.

verses.restored: While Israel was dwelling in that land, Reuben violated the household boundary with Bilhah, his father’s concubine, and Israel heard. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when Israel dwelt in that land, that Reuben went...

notes.title: Reuben and Bilhah
notes.body: The source is brief but grave; later tradition treats this as a serious challenge to the father’s household authority and Reuben’s standing.

verses.restored: The sons of Leah: Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.

familiar.text: The sons of Leah; Reuben...

verses.restored: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

familiar.text: The sons of Rachel; Joseph, and Benjamin:

verses.restored: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel’s slave-woman: Dan and Naphtali.

familiar.text: And the sons of Bilhah, Rachel's handmaid...

verses.restored: The sons of Zilpah, Leah’s slave-woman: Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan-Aram.

familiar.text: And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's handmaid...

verses.restored: Jacob came to Isaac his father at Mamre, to Kiriath-arba, that is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac had sojourned.

familiar.text: And Jacob came unto Isaac his father unto Mamre...

verses.restored: The days of Isaac were one hundred eighty years.

familiar.text: And the days of Isaac were an hundred and fourscore years.

verses.restored: Isaac expired and died and was gathered to his people, old and full of days. Esau and Jacob his sons buried him.

familiar.text: And Isaac gave up the ghost, and died...
